content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
f (916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Andrew Morris 
Town Attorney 
Town of Mammoth Lakes 
P .0. Box 1609 
Mammoth Lakes, CA 93546 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-17-142 
Dear Mr. Morris: 
June 22, 2017 
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Greg Eckert, a member of 
the Town of Mammoth Lakes' Planning and Economic Development Commission, regarding the 
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 You recently notified the 
Commission that the government decision in question occurred on June 14, 2017 without 
Mr. Eckert's participation, and the requested advice is no longer needed. Accordingly, we 
acknowledge that you withdraw your request for advice. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
LKH:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Jack C. Woodside 
General Counsel 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC acknowledges withdrawal of the advice request because the government decision occurred on June 14, 2017 without Mr. Eckert's participation, making the requested advice no longer necessary.

embedding.summary: This is a withdrawal letter from the FPPC regarding an advice request from the Town Attorney of Mammoth Lakes concerning a potential conflict of interest issue. The request was withdrawn after the underlying government decision was made without the subject individual's participation.
